LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JULY 14, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE MS. WOODS THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MS. WOODS MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JULY 3, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE DEMURRER WILL BE SUSTAINED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. (302)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE AMENDED COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES FACTS THAT, IF BELIEVED BY THE FINDER OF FACT, COULD SUPPORT A CONCLUSION THAT DEFENDANT IS ESTOPPED FROM RAISING THE 6-MONTH STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS OF GOV'T CODE SEC. 945.6. Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On Plaintiff General Negligence Cause Of Action (Second Cause Of Action)
Off Calendar Dec-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. ERIC TOSARIS OF THE LAW OFFICES OF IAN PAUL ZIMMERMAN, TEL. NO. 1-415-887-9394 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DAVID DELBON, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-3962 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND NATHANIEL TAN'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On Plaintiff General Negligence Cause Of Action (Second Cause Of Action) is DENIED. It is true that a common law claim for negligence cannot be asserted against the City. However, in reading the complaint liberally per CCP 452 the Court construes a cause of action under Government Code 815.2 and 820(a). Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Kelley DeYoung, CSR #6780 =302/ru

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-14-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. The amended complaint sufficiently alleges facts that, if believed by the finder of fact, could support a conclusion that defendant is estopped from raising the 6-month statute of limitations of Gov't Code sec. 945.6. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/pjb

MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to July 14, 2014 to give Ms. Woods the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Ms. Woods must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 3, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the demurrer will be sustained. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
